---
title: "testlyrics"
author: "Jack Schloesser"
date: "2026-04-21"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
install.packages(c("rvest", "httr", "dplyr", "stringr"))
install.packages("rlang")
```

```{r}
library(rvest)
library(httr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
```

```{r}
song_urls <- c(
  "https://genius.com/Olivia-rodrigo-drop-dead-lyrics",
  "https://genius.com/Katseye-pinky-up-lyrics",
  "https://genius.com/Kendrick-lamar-and-sza-luther-lyrics",
  "https://genius.com/Rose-and-bruno-mars-apt-lyrics",
  "https://genius.com/Lady-gaga-and-bruno-mars-die-with-a-smile-lyrics"
)

get_lyrics <- function(url) {
  tryCatch({
    page <- rvest::read_html(url)
    
    lyrics_nodes <- rvest::html_elements(page, "[data-lyrics-container='true']")
    
    lyrics <- lyrics_nodes %>%
      rvest::html_text() %>%
      paste(collapse = "\n")
    
    lyrics <- lyrics %>%
      stringr::str_remove_all("\\[.*?\\]") %>%              # remove [Verse], etc.
      stringr::str_remove_all("(?i)translations.*") %>%     # remove translations
      stringr::str_remove_all("(?i)contributors.*") %>%     # remove contributors text
      stringr::str_remove_all("^\\d+\\s+") %>%              # remove leading number
      stringr::str_replace_all("([a-z])([A-Z])", "\\1 \\2") %>% #inserts space where sometimes words combine
      stringr::str_replace_all("([0-9])([A-Za-z])", "\\1 \\2") %>% #same as above except for numbers after words
      stringr::str_replace_all("([A-Za-z])([0-9])", "\\1 \\2") %>% # vice-versa as above
      
      stringr::str_squish() #cleans whitespacae
    
    return(lyrics)
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    return(NA)
  })
}

lyrics_df <- data.frame(
  url = song_urls,
  lyrics = sapply(song_urls, get_lyrics),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

lyrics_to_words <- function(song_name, lyrics_text) {
  
  words <- lyrics_text %>%
    stringr::str_to_lower() %>%
    stringr::str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9\\s']", " ") %>%  # remove punctuation
    stringr::str_squish() %>%
    stringr::str_split("\\s+") %>%
    unlist()
  
  data.frame(
    song = song_name,
    word = words,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

word_df <- do.call(
  rbind,
  lapply(seq_len(nrow(lyrics_df)), function(i) {
    lyrics_to_words(
      song_name = lyrics_df$url[i],
      lyrics_text = lyrics_df$lyrics[i]
    )
  })
)

print(word_df)
```
